titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in 2106
GA 2106
The New Testament
Das Neue Testament
The Gospel according to John
john

respStmt.name: the International Greek New Testament Project

titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0): available for re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators (the IGNTP and any named participants) and an indication is given of any changes made. See further .

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by the International Greek New Testament Project in its work towards the Editio Critica Maior of John between 2010 and 2013.

editorialDecl.p: The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE, later converted to XML. The original transcription guidelines can be found at
The plain text file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used are version 1.4 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: Correction removed at 11.22 by David Parker.
Transcription changed at 18.34 by David Parker.
Transcription changed at 18.28 by David Parker.
αυτον removed at 18.13 by David Parker.
εκραυγαξεν to εκραυγαζεν at 11.43 by Rachel Kevern.
Word break corrected at 12.1 by David Parker.
Iota adscripts removed by David Parker.
Word breaks in 10,40, 12.1, 18.40 and 20.16 corrected, and nomen sacrum added in 19.32 by David Parker.
Adjusted to XML specifications 1.4 by Hugh Houghton.
Converted from plain text by Catherine Smith using Python conversion scripts 1.0.

body.note: Status:G-A Number: 2106Name of book transcribed: JohnFirst folio transcribed: 71vBase text used: TRns Transcribed by: E.P.Transcription begun: 8 Nov 2011Transcription finished: 13 Nov 2011 Does this manuscript contain (delete and complete as appropriate): Lectionary annotations - Yes Initials and other enlarged letters - Yes Diples (> or >> in the margin next to certain text, indicating that text is a quotation from the Old Testament) - No Are there any special symbols in the margin next to any of the following verses: 5.4 No 7.53 - 8.11 No Other remarks:Repeated verses:1.3 on F75r1.6 on F76r17.17-19 on F259r Omitted verses:2.24-3.23.36 5.12 7.53-8.11 8.589.22-23 11.46 12.37 14.14 16.14Heavily abbreviated. Numerous spelling mistakes. Iota adscript used. FINAL plain text transcription date: 24.04.2103

ab.note: Instead of οφθαλμους, there is a drawing of 2 eyes
